stem: Let \( A \) be the matrix \[ A = \begin{bmatrix} 1 & [[s]] & [[t]] & [[m14]] \\ [[p]] & [[m22]] & [[m23]] & [[m24]] \\ [[q]] & [[m32]] & [[m33]] & [[m34]] \end{bmatrix}. \] What is the rank of \( A \) (the dimension of its row space)?
solution: Row-reduce \( A \). The number of nonzero (pivot) rows is the rank, which is \( [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This is the nullity \( 4 - \text{rank} \) (the number of free columns), not the rank itself.
This is the number of columns; the rank cannot exceed the number of rows here.
This undercounts the pivots by one.
This overcounts the pivots by one.
This would require every row to be a multiple of a single row.
This assumes the rows are all independent without checking for dependence.
This overcounts the pivots.
This miscounts the number of independent rows.

stem: For the matrix \[ B = \begin{bmatrix} 1 & [[s]] & [[t]] & [[m14]] \\ [[p]] & [[m22]] & [[m23]] & [[m24]] \\ [[q]] & [[m32]] & [[m33]] & [[m34]] \end{bmatrix}, \] compute the nullity of \( B \) (the dimension of the set of solutions to \( B\mathbf{x} = \mathbf{0} \)).
solution: \( B \) has \( 4 \) columns and rank \( [[rankval]] \). By the rank–nullity theorem, \( \text{nullity}(B) = 4 - \operatorname{rank}(B) = 4 - [[rankval]] = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This is the rank of \( B \), not the nullity; rank and nullity are swapped here.
This is the number of columns, as if the rank were zero.
This assumes every column is a pivot column.
This overcounts the free columns by one.
This undercounts the free columns by one.
This miscounts the number of pivot columns.
This miscounts the number of free columns.
This overcounts the free columns.

stem: The linear map \( T : \mathbb{R}^3 \to \mathbb{R}^3 \) is given by \[ T(x, y, z) = \big([[a]]x + [[b]]y + [[c]]z,\ [[d]]x + [[e]]y + [[f]]z,\ [[g]]x + [[h]]y + [[k]]z\big). \] What is the rank of \( T \) (the dimension of its image)?
solution: The rank of \( T \) equals the rank of its coefficient matrix, formed from the coefficients in each output coordinate. Row-reducing gives rank \( [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This is the nullity, not the rank; they are swapped.
This assumes \( T \) is always invertible without checking.
This assumes every row is a multiple of another.
This overcounts the independent rows by one.
This undercounts the independent rows by one.
This undercounts the rank.
This is a plausible but incorrect intermediate rank.
This overcounts the rank.
